original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Stroke and transient ischaemic attack in over 16s: diagnosis and initial management
original_registry_entry.population: People aged over 16 years with suspected or confirmed stroke or transient ischaemic attack.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Initial assessment, specialist TIA assessment, imaging, and secondary-prevention planning.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for TIA specialist assessment, imaging, and secondary-prevention documentation; partial for a broad long-term stroke or TIA follow-up workflow and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.1.7 — secondary prevention after confirmed TIA
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led secondary-prevention planning after specialist confirmation of TIA without mapping medicine names or doses.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.4 — brain and carotid imaging after specialist TIA assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed MRI and carotid imaging in the specified specialist-assessment context.

title: Stroke and transient ischaemic attack in over 16s: diagnosis and initial management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: People aged over 16 years with suspected or confirmed stroke or transient ischaemic attack.
intended_setting: Initial assessment, specialist TIA assessment, imaging, and secondary-prevention planning.
